probability.q: Roll a fair die 4 times. P(exactly one six)?
probability.ex: Choose which of the 4 rolls is the six and let the rest miss: 4·5^3 / 6^4 = 125/324.

probability.q: 4 people sit in a row in random order. P(two particular people are adjacent)?
probability.ex: Glue the pair into one block: 2 · 3! / 4! = 2/4 = 1/2.

probability.q: A fair 20-sided die has faces 1 through 20. Expected value of one roll?
5 people sit in a row in random order. P(two particular people are adjacent)?
probability.ex: Glue the pair into one block: 2 · 4! / 5! = 2/5 = 2/5.

probability.q: Roll a fair die 3 times. P(at least one six)?
probability.ex: 1 − (5/6)^3 = 91/216. The complement (no six) is 125/216.

probability.q: A fair 6-sided die has faces 1 through 6. Expected value of one roll?
A fair 8-sided die has faces 1 through 8. Expected value of one roll?
You roll a fair 12-sided die until a chosen face appears. Expected number of rolls?
probability.ex: Geometric with success probability 1/12, so the expected wait is 12.

probability.q: Roll a fair die 4 times. P(at least one six)?
probability.ex: 1 − (5/6)^4 = 671/1296. The complement (no six) is 625/1296.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 4 times. P(exactly 3 heads)?
8 people sit in a row in random order. P(two particular people are adjacent)?
probability.ex: Glue the pair into one block: 2 · 7! / 8! = 2/8 = 1/4.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 7)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 6 sum to 7, so 1/6.

probability.q: A fair 10-sided die has faces 1 through 10. Expected value of one roll?
Flip a fair coin 4 times. P(exactly 1 heads)?
Roll a fair die 2 times. P(at least one six)?
probability.ex: 1 − (5/6)^2 = 11/36. The complement (no six) is 25/36.

probability.q: You roll a fair 6-sided die until a chosen face appears. Expected number of rolls?
probability.ex: Geometric with success probability 1/6, so the expected wait is 6.

probability.q: A fair 4-sided die has faces 1 through 4. Expected value of one roll?
Roll a fair die 3 times. P(exactly one six)?
probability.ex: Choose which of the 3 rolls is the six and let the rest miss: 3·5^2 / 6^3 = 25/72.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 9)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 4 sum to 9, so 1/9.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 5)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 4 sum to 5, so 1/9.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 3 times. P(exactly 1 heads)?
Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 3)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 2 sum to 3, so 1/18.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 2)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 1 sum to 2, so 1/36.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 10)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 3 sum to 10, so 1/12.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 5 times. P(exactly 4 heads)?
Flip a fair coin 6 times. P(exactly 5 heads)?
7 people sit in a row in random order. P(two particular people are adjacent)?
probability.ex: Glue the pair into one block: 2 · 6! / 7! = 2/7 = 2/7.

probability.q: You roll a fair 4-sided die until a chosen face appears. Expected number of rolls?
probability.ex: Geometric with success probability 1/4, so the expected wait is 4.

probability.q: 9 people sit in a row in random order. P(two particular people are adjacent)?
probability.ex: Glue the pair into one block: 2 · 8! / 9! = 2/9 = 2/9.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 12)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 1 sum to 12, so 1/36.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 3 times. P(exactly 3 heads)?
6 people sit in a row in random order. P(two particular people are adjacent)?
probability.ex: Glue the pair into one block: 2 · 5! / 6! = 2/6 = 1/3.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 6)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 5 sum to 6, so 5/36.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 4 times. P(exactly 2 heads)?
Roll a fair die 2 times. P(exactly one six)?
probability.ex: Choose which of the 2 rolls is the six and let the rest miss: 2·5^1 / 6^2 = 5/18.

probability.q: Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 11)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 2 sum to 11, so 1/18.

probability.q: You roll a fair 10-sided die until a chosen face appears. Expected number of rolls?
probability.ex: Geometric with success probability 1/10, so the expected wait is 10.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 5 times. P(exactly 1 heads)?
Roll two fair dice. P(the sum equals 8)?
probability.ex: Of 36 equally likely ordered rolls, 5 sum to 8, so 5/36.

probability.q: Flip a fair coin 4 times. P(exactly 0 heads)?
